speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Good morrow, and well met. How have you done

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Since last we saw in France?

speaker: NORFOLK
text: I thank your Grace,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Healthful, and ever since a fresh admirer

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Of what I saw there.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: An untimely ague

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Stayed me a prisoner in my chamber when

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Those suns of glory, those two lights of men,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Met in the vale of Andren.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: ’Twixt Guynes and Arde.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: I was then present, saw them salute on horseback,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Beheld them when they lighted, how they clung

speaker: NORFOLK
text: In their embracement, as they grew together—

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Which had they, what four throned ones could have weighed

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Such a compounded one?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: All the whole time

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I was my chamber’s prisoner.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Then you lost

speaker: NORFOLK
text: The view of earthly glory. Men might say

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Till this time pomp was single, but now married

speaker: NORFOLK
text: To one above itself. Each following day

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Became the next day’s master, till the last

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Made former wonders its. Today the French,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: All clinquant, all in gold, like heathen gods,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Shone down the English, and tomorrow they

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Made Britain India: every man that stood

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Showed like a mine. Their dwarfish pages were

speaker: NORFOLK
text: As cherubins, all gilt. The madams too,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Not used to toil, did almost sweat to bear

speaker: NORFOLK
text: The pride upon them, that their very labor

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Was to them as a painting. Now this masque

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Was cried incomparable; and th’ ensuing night

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Made it a fool and beggar. The two kings,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Equal in luster, were now best, now worst,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: As presence did present them: him in eye

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Still him in praise; and being present both,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: ’Twas said they saw but one, and no discerner

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Durst wag his tongue in censure. When these suns—

speaker: NORFOLK
text: For so they phrase ’em—by their heralds challenged

speaker: NORFOLK
text: The noble spirits to arms, they did perform

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Beyond thought’s compass, that former fabulous story,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Being now seen possible enough, got credit

speaker: NORFOLK
text: That Bevis was believed.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: O, you go far.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: As I belong to worship, and affect

speaker: NORFOLK
text: In honor honesty, the tract of everything

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Would by a good discourser lose some life

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Which action’s self was tongue to. All was royal;

speaker: NORFOLK
text: To the disposing of it naught rebelled.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Order gave each thing view. The office did

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Distinctly his full function.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Who did guide,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I mean who set the body and the limbs

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Of this great sport together, as you guess?

speaker: NORFOLK
text: One, certes, that promises no element

speaker: NORFOLK
text: In such a business.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I pray you who, my lord?

speaker: NORFOLK
text: All this was ordered by the good discretion

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Of the right reverend Cardinal of York.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The devil speed him! No man’s pie is freed

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: From his ambitious finger. What had he

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To do in these fierce vanities? I wonder

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: That such a keech can with his very bulk

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Take up the rays o’ th’ beneficial sun

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And keep it from the Earth.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Surely, sir,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: There’s in him stuff that puts him to these ends;

speaker: NORFOLK
text: For, being not propped by ancestry, whose grace

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Chalks successors their way, nor called upon

speaker: NORFOLK
text: For high feats done to th’ crown, neither allied

speaker: NORFOLK
text: To eminent assistants, but spiderlike,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Out of his self-drawing web, he gives us note

speaker: NORFOLK
text: The force of his own merit makes his way—

speaker: NORFOLK
text: A gift that heaven gives for him which buys

speaker: NORFOLK
text: A place next to the King.

speaker: ABERGAVENNY
text: I cannot tell

speaker: ABERGAVENNY
text: What heaven hath given him—let some graver eye

speaker: ABERGAVENNY
text: Pierce into that—but I can see his pride

speaker: ABERGAVENNY
text: Peep through each part of him. Whence has he that?

speaker: ABERGAVENNY
text: If not from hell, the devil is a niggard,

speaker: ABERGAVENNY
text: Or has given all before, and he begins

speaker: ABERGAVENNY
text: A new hell in himself.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Why the devil,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Upon this French going-out, took he upon him,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Without the privity o’ th’ King, t’ appoint

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Who should attend on him? He makes up the file

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Of all the gentry, for the most part such

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To whom as great a charge as little honor

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: He meant to lay upon; and his own letter,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The honorable board of council out,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Must fetch him in he papers.

speaker: ABERGAVENNY
text: I do know

speaker: ABERGAVENNY
text: Kinsmen of mine, three at the least, that have

speaker: ABERGAVENNY
text: By this so sickened their estates that never

speaker: ABERGAVENNY
text: They shall abound as formerly.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: O, many

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Have broke their backs with laying manors on ’em

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: For this great journey. What did this vanity

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: But minister communication of

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: A most poor issue?

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Grievingly I think

speaker: NORFOLK
text: The peace between the French and us not values

speaker: NORFOLK
text: The cost that did conclude it.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Every man,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: After the hideous storm that followed, was

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: A thing inspired and, not consulting, broke

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Into a general prophecy: that this tempest,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Dashing the garment of this peace, aboded

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The sudden breach on ’t.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Which is budded out,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: For France hath flawed the league and hath attached

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Our merchants’ goods at Bordeaux.

speaker: ABERGAVENNY
text: Is it therefore

speaker: ABERGAVENNY
text: Th’ ambassador is silenced?

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Marry, is ’t.

speaker: ABERGAVENNY
text: A proper title of a peace, and purchased

speaker: ABERGAVENNY
text: At a superfluous rate!

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Why, all this business

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Our reverend cardinal carried.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Like it your Grace,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: The state takes notice of the private difference

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Betwixt you and the Cardinal. I advise you—

speaker: NORFOLK
text: And take it from a heart that wishes towards you

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Honor and plenteous safety—that you read

speaker: NORFOLK
text: The Cardinal’s malice and his potency

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Together; to consider further that

speaker: NORFOLK
text: What his high hatred would effect wants not

speaker: NORFOLK
text: A minister in his power. You know his nature,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: That he’s revengeful, and I know his sword

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Hath a sharp edge; it’s long, and ’t may be said

speaker: NORFOLK
text: It reaches far, and where ’twill not extend,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Thither he darts it. Bosom up my counsel;

speaker: NORFOLK
text: You’ll find it wholesome. Lo where comes that rock

speaker: NORFOLK
text: That I advise your shunning.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: The Duke of Buckingham’s surveyor, ha?

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Where’s his examination?

speaker: SECRETARY
text: Here, so please you.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Is he in person ready?

speaker: SECRETARY
text: Ay, please your Grace.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Well, we shall then know more, and Buckingham

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Shall lessen this big look.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: This butcher’s cur is venomed-mouthed, and I

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Have not the power to muzzle him; therefore best

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Not wake him in his slumber. A beggar’s book

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Outworths a noble’s blood.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: What, are you chafed?

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Ask God for temp’rance. That’s th’ appliance only

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Which your disease requires.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I read in ’s looks

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Matter against me, and his eye reviled

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Me as his abject object. At this instant

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: He bores me with some trick. He’s gone to th’ King.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I’ll follow and outstare him.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Stay, my lord,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: And let your reason with your choler question

speaker: NORFOLK
text: What ’tis you go about. To climb steep hills

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Requires slow pace at first. Anger is like

speaker: NORFOLK
text: A full hot horse who, being allowed his way,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Self-mettle tires him. Not a man in England

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Can advise me like you; be to yourself

speaker: NORFOLK
text: As you would to your friend.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I’ll to the King,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And from a mouth of honor quite cry down

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: This Ipswich fellow’s insolence, or proclaim

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: There’s difference in no persons.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Be advised.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Heat not a furnace for your foe so hot

speaker: NORFOLK
text: That it do singe yourself. We may outrun

speaker: NORFOLK
text: By violent swiftness that which we run at

speaker: NORFOLK
text: And lose by overrunning. Know you not

speaker: NORFOLK
text: The fire that mounts the liquor till ’t run o’er

speaker: NORFOLK
text: In seeming to augment it wastes it? Be advised.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: I say again there is no English soul

speaker: NORFOLK
text: More stronger to direct you than yourself,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: If with the sap of reason you would quench

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Or but allay the fire of passion.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Sir,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I am thankful to you, and I’ll go along

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: By your prescription. But this top-proud fellow—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Whom from the flow of gall I name not, but

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: From sincere motions—by intelligence,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And proofs as clear as founts in July when

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: We see each grain of gravel, I do know

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To be corrupt and treasonous.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Say not “treasonous.”

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To th’ King I’ll say ’t, and make my vouch as strong

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: As shore of rock. Attend. This holy fox,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Or wolf, or both—for he is equal rav’nous

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: As he is subtle, and as prone to mischief

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: As able to perform ’t, his mind and place

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Infecting one another, yea reciprocally—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Only to show his pomp as well in France

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: As here at home, suggests the King our master

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To this last costly treaty, th’ interview

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: That swallowed so much treasure and like a glass

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Did break i’ th’ rinsing.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Faith, and so it did.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Pray give me favor, sir. This cunning cardinal

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The articles o’ th’ combination drew

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: As himself pleased; and they were ratified

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: As he cried “Thus let be,” to as much end

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: As give a crutch to th’ dead. But our Count Cardinal

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Has done this, and ’tis well, for worthy Wolsey,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Who cannot err, he did it. Now this follows—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Which, as I take it, is a kind of puppy

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To th’ old dam treason: Charles the Emperor,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Under pretense to see the Queen his aunt—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: For ’twas indeed his color, but he came

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To whisper Wolsey—here makes visitation;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: His fears were that the interview betwixt

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: England and France might through their amity

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Breed him some prejudice, for from this league

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Peeped harms that menaced him; privily

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Deals with our cardinal and, as I trow—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Which I do well, for I am sure the Emperor

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Paid ere he promised, whereby his suit was granted

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Ere it was asked. But when the way was made

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And paved with gold, the Emperor thus desired

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: That he would please to alter the King’s course

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And break the foresaid peace. Let the King know—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: As soon he shall by me—that thus the Cardinal

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Does buy and sell his honor as he pleases

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And for his own advantage.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: I am sorry

speaker: NORFOLK
text: To hear this of him, and could wish he were

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Something mistaken in ’t.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: No, not a syllable.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I do pronounce him in that very shape

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: He shall appear in proof.

speaker: BRANDON
text: Your office, Sergeant: execute it.

speaker: SERGEANT
text: Sir,

speaker: SERGEANT
text: My lord the Duke of Buckingham and Earl

speaker: SERGEANT
text: Of Hertford, Stafford, and Northampton, I

speaker: SERGEANT
text: Arrest thee of high treason, in the name

speaker: SERGEANT
text: Of our most sovereign king.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Lo you, my lord,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The net has fall’n upon me. I shall perish

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Under device and practice.

speaker: BRANDON
text: I am sorry

speaker: BRANDON
text: To see you ta’en from liberty, to look on

speaker: BRANDON
text: The business present. ’Tis his Highness’ pleasure

speaker: BRANDON
text: You shall to th’ Tower.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: It will help me nothing

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: To plead mine innocence, for that dye is on me

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Which makes my whit’st part black. The will of heaven

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Be done in this and all things. I obey.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: O my Lord Abergavenny, fare you well.

speaker: BRANDON
text: Nay, he must bear you company.—The King

speaker: BRANDON
text: Is pleased you shall to th’ Tower, till you know

speaker: BRANDON
text: How he determines further.

speaker: ABERGAVENNY
text: As the Duke said,

speaker: ABERGAVENNY
text: The will of heaven be done, and the King’s pleasure

speaker: ABERGAVENNY
text: By me obeyed.

speaker: BRANDON
text: Here is a warrant from

speaker: BRANDON
text: The King t’ attach Lord Mountacute, and the bodies

speaker: BRANDON
text: Of the Duke’s confessor, John de la Car,

speaker: BRANDON
text: One Gilbert Peck, his counselor—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: So, so;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: These are the limbs o’ th’ plot. No more, I hope.

speaker: BRANDON
text: A monk o’ th’ Chartreux.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: O, Michael Hopkins?

speaker: BRANDON
text: He.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: My surveyor is false. The o’ergreat cardinal

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Hath showed him gold. My life is spanned already.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I am the shadow of poor Buckingham,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Whose figure even this instant cloud puts on

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text:
By dark’ning my clear sun.
                 
                To
                 
                Norfolk
                .
               My lord, farewell.
